description: Add --chain flag to batch mode that makes sequential PRDs build on each other via chained git worktrees

criteria: Rust: No warnings from cargo check or cargo clippy
Rust: All tests pass with cargo test
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless required
Default behavior (without --chain) must be identical to current behavior

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Approach before code. Anticipate edge cases. Consider 2-3 approaches, pick the best
2. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable code, wired in according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS — Self-critique after code. Compiles, type-checks, all tests pass
4. CODE QUALITY — Quality dimensions explicit. Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — read qualityDimensions on every task; know what 'good' looks like before coding
Approach before code — consider 2-3 approaches with tradeoffs, pick the best, then implement
Each task delivers working code + tests together
Self-critique after code — review your own implementation for correctness, style, and performance before moving on
Test boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context

acceptanceCriteria: Signature: ensure_worktree(project_root, branch_name, yes_mode, start_point: Option<&str>)
When start_point is Some and branch is NEW: `git worktree add -b <branch> <path> -- <start_point>` is executed
When start_point is None and branch is NEW: `git worktree add -b <branch> <path>` (no `--`, no start_point) — identical to current behavior
When branch already EXISTS: start_point is ignored, existing `git worktree add <path> <branch>` path unchanged
Security: `--` separator always inserted before start_point to prevent flag injection
All existing callers updated to pass None — callers: engine.rs line ~1105, all test call sites in worktree.rs
Unit test: ensure_worktree with start_point=Some('branch-a') creates new branch containing branch-a's commits
Unit test: ensure_worktree with start_point=None creates new branch from HEAD (regression test)
Unit test: ensure_worktree with start_point=Some for an EXISTING branch ignores start_point
Negative: Must NOT change the git command for the existing-branch path (line ~262)
Known-bad: if `--` separator is omitted, a start_point of '--exec=something' could be interpreted as a git flag — test verifies args structure
userStories.description:
Add an optional `start_point: Option<&str>` parameter to `ensure_worktree()` in worktree.rs. When creating a NEW branch (branch doesn't exist), pass the start_point to `git worktree add -b <branch> <path> -- <start_point>`. The `--` separator is critical to prevent flag injection from malicious branch names.

DO: Add the parameter, use `--` separator before start_point in git args, update ALL callers to pass None.
DO NOT: Change behavior when start_point is None — it must be identical to current behavior. Do NOT touch the existing-branch path (start_point is irrelevant when branch already exists).

Edge cases:
- start_point=None: branches from HEAD (current behavior, unchanged)
- start_point=Some(valid_ref): new branch from that ref
- start_point=Some(invalid_ref): git error propagated cleanly
- start_point looks like a flag ("--exec=bad"): `--` separator prevents injection
- Branch already exists: start_point ignored entirely

Known-bad to guard against:
- Forgetting the `--` separator would allow start_point values starting with `-` to be interpreted as git flags
edgeCases: start_point=None: identical to current behavior (branches from HEAD)
start_point=Some(valid_branch): new branch created from that ref
start_point=Some(invalid_ref): git error propagated via TaskMgrError::InvalidState
Branch already exists + start_point=Some: start_point silently ignored

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Git error propagated as TaskMgrError::InvalidState with stderr message
-- separator ensures it's treated as a ref, not a flag

userStories.notes: Key function: ensure_worktree() at worktree.rs:116. New-branch creation at line 282. Existing-branch path at line 262. Test helper: setup_git_repo_with_file() in test_utils.rs. Existing test pattern: test_ensure_worktree_creates_new_worktree at line 494. To verify start_point works: create branch-a with a commit, then create branch-b with start_point=branch-a, check git log in branch-b contains branch-a's commit.

correctness: start_point=None must produce EXACTLY the same git command as before (no -- added)
start_point=Some must include -- separator before the value
Existing-branch path must be completely untouched
performance: No performance considerations — git commands are the bottleneck
style: Follow existing pattern of building args vec then calling Command::new('git').args(&args)
Keep the if/else structure for branch_exists vs !branch_exists

userStories.title: Add start_point parameter to ensure_worktree

acceptanceCriteria: LoopRunConfig has `pub chain_base: Option<String>` field with doc comment
LoopResult has `pub branch_name: Option<String>` field with doc comment
engine.rs passes run_config.chain_base.as_deref() as the start_point arg to ensure_worktree()
branch_name is set from prd_metadata.branch_name (around line 1068) on the success path
All early-return LoopResult constructions set branch_name: None (grep for 'LoopResult {' in engine.rs)
All LoopRunConfig construction sites updated to include chain_base: None — main.rs Run command (~line 621)
Negative: engine.rs must NOT contain any chain-specific logic (no if-chain checks) — it's pure plumbing
Compiles: cargo check passes with all callers updated
userStories.description:
Thread the chain mechanism through the engine layer. Add `chain_base: Option<String>` to LoopRunConfig (the batch runner sets this) and `branch_name: Option<String>` to LoopResult (the batch runner reads this to advance the chain).

DO: Add both fields, pass chain_base.as_deref() to ensure_worktree, set branch_name from prd_metadata.branch_name in ALL LoopResult return paths.
DO NOT: Add any chain logic to engine.rs — it's just plumbing. The engine doesn't know about chaining.

Edge cases:
- chain_base=None: ensure_worktree gets None start_point (current behavior)
- branch_name populated from prd_metadata even when chain is not active (always useful metadata)

Known-bad to guard against:
- Forgetting to set branch_name on early-return error paths in run_loop — ALL return paths must set it
edgeCases: chain_base=None: start_point passed as None to ensure_worktree (no behavioral change)
No branch_name in prd_metadata: branch_name in LoopResult is None
Early error return: branch_name is None (haven't read metadata yet)

failureModes.cause: Missing chain_base field on a LoopRunConfig construction site
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Compile error — struct literal missing field

userStories.notes: LoopRunConfig at engine.rs:701. LoopResult at engine.rs:689. ensure_worktree call at engine.rs:1105. prd_metadata.branch_name read at engine.rs:1068. Grep for 'LoopResult {' to find ALL return sites — there are several early returns. main.rs constructs LoopRunConfig at ~line 621 (Run command) and batch.rs constructs it at ~line 321.

correctness: Every LoopResult return path must set branch_name (None for errors, Some for success)
chain_base=None must produce identical behavior to pre-change code
performance: No performance impact — just threading Option values
style: Doc comments on both new fields explaining their purpose and who sets/reads them
chain_base doc: 'Set by --chain in batch mode. Passed to ensure_worktree as start_point.'
branch_name doc: 'Branch used by this run. Read by batch --chain to advance the chain.'

userStories.title: Add chain_base to LoopRunConfig and branch_name to LoopResult

acceptanceCriteria: Batch variant has `chain: bool` field with #[arg(long, default_value_t = false)]
Doc comment: explains chaining behavior, mentions stop-on-failure, mentions requires worktrees

main.rs Commands::Batch destructure includes `chain`
main.rs passes `chain` to run_batch() call (run_batch doesn't accept it yet — add a TODO comment or adjust signature)
CLI test: --chain flag parses as true
CLI test: --chain defaults to false when omitted
CLI test: --chain combined with --yes and --keep-worktrees all parse correctly
Compiles: cargo check passes (may need temporary `let _ = chain;` in main.rs if run_batch signature not yet updated)
userStories.description:
Add `--chain` boolean flag to the Batch CLI variant in commands.rs. Thread it through main.rs dispatch to the run_batch call. Add CLI parsing tests.

DO: Add the flag with clear doc comment and help text. Update after_help examples. Add tests for flag parsing.
DO NOT: Implement any chain logic — this is just the CLI surface. The flag is passed to run_batch which doesn't use it yet (CHAIN-004).

Edge cases:
- --chain not specified: defaults to false
- --chain with --keep-worktrees: valid combination, both flags passed through

Known-bad to guard against:
- Forgetting to update the Commands::Batch destructure pattern in main.rs — causes compile error (good, but fix it)
edgeCases: --chain with all other batch flags: all parse independently

failureModes.cause: Forgot to destructure chain in main.rs

userStories.notes: Batch variant at commands.rs:734. Main.rs dispatch at line 676. Existing CLI test pattern at cli/tests.rs:2411 (test_batch_with_pattern_and_yes). Follow the exact same pattern for the --chain tests. The run_batch signature will be updated in CHAIN-004; for now, either update signature proactively or use `let _ = chain;` to suppress unused warning.

correctness: Parsing must not affect other Batch flags
style: Follow exact same pattern as keep_worktrees flag (#[arg(long, default_value_t = false)])
Tests follow existing test_batch_* pattern in cli/tests.rs

userStories.title: Add --chain CLI flag and thread through main.rs

acceptanceCriteria: run_batch signature includes `chain: bool` parameter
Upfront validation when chain=true: calls read_branch_name_from_prd for each PRD file before the loop starts
Upfront validation: returns error (BatchResult with failed=1) if any PRD lacks a branchName
Upfront validation: prints warning to stderr if duplicate branchName values found across PRDs
Chain tracking: chain_base starts as None, advances to loop_result.branch_name after each PRD
Chain tracking: LoopRunConfig.chain_base set from chain_base when chain=true, None when chain=false
Stop-on-failure: when chain=true and exit_code!=0, remaining PRDs are marked skipped and loop breaks
Stop-on-failure: stderr message 'Chain stopped: PRD failed, skipping remaining PRDs'
PrdRunResult has branch_name: Option<String> and chain_base: Option<String> fields
Summary output when chain=true shows chain info: '[OK] phase-1.json → feat/phase-1 (from HEAD)'
Cleanup diagnostic: when worktree removed mid-chain, prints 'Worktree removed but branch X retained for chaining'
chain=false: no validation, no tracking, no stop-on-failure — ZERO behavioral change
Negative: chain advancement must use loop_result.branch_name, NOT read_branch_name_from_prd()
Known-bad: if chain=false path accidentally runs validation, it would fail on PRDs without branchName — test chain=false with a branchless PRD to verify
Unit test: validate_chain_branches errors on missing branchName
Unit test: validate_chain_branches warns on duplicate branchNames
Unit test: stop-on-failure skips remaining PRDs after a failure
userStories.description:
This is the core orchestration task. Add `chain: bool` parameter to `run_batch()`. When chain=true:
1. Upfront validation: read all PRD branchNames, error if any missing, warn on duplicates
2. Chain tracking: use LoopResult.branch_name to advance chain_base after each PRD
3. Stop-on-failure: if a PRD fails, skip remaining PRDs
4. Chain-aware summary: show 'from HEAD' / 'from feat/phase-1' in output
5. Cleanup diagnostic: when worktree cleaned up mid-chain, print 'branch retained for chaining'

DO: Use `status_queries::read_branch_name_from_prd()` for upfront validation. Use `loop_result.branch_name` (not pre-read) for chain advancement. Update PrdRunResult with branch_name and chain_base fields.
DO NOT: Modify engine.rs or worktree.rs — those are done in CHAIN-001/002.

Edge cases:
- First PRD: chain_base=None (branches from HEAD)
- All PRDs same branchName: warn but allow (worktree reused, changes accumulate)
- PRD failure: remaining PRDs skipped with clear message
- chain=false: completely unchanged behavior — no validation, no tracking, no stop-on-failure

Known-bad to guard against:
- Using pre-read branch name (from read_branch_name_from_prd) for chain advancement instead of LoopResult.branch_name — could mismatch if DB normalizes the name
- Forgetting to update PrdRunResult construction sites with new fields
edgeCases: First PRD in chain: chain_base=None (branches from HEAD)
All PRDs same branchName + chain=true: warn, but worktree reuses correctly
PRD without branchName + chain=true: caught by upfront validation
PRD failure + chain=true: remaining skipped, chain_base still valid for diagnostics
chain=false: no validation, no tracking, no stop-on-failure

failureModes.expectedBehavior: read_branch_name_from_prd returns None, treated as 'missing branchName' → error
Remaining PRDs skipped, batch summary shows failure + skipped counts

failureModes.cause: LoopResult.branch_name is None for a completed PRD
failureModes.expectedBehavior: chain_base stays at previous value (chain from last known branch)

userStories.notes: run_batch at batch.rs:197. Loop at line 284. LoopRunConfig construction at line 321. PrdRunResult at line 30. read_branch_name_from_prd in status_queries.rs:63. The validation function can be a private fn validate_chain_branches(pairs: &[(PathBuf, PathBuf)]) -> TaskMgrResult<()>. For unit tests: create temp files with/without branchName JSON and call the validation function directly. The stop-on-failure test can verify the results vec structure without actually calling run_loop.

correctness: chain=false must be a complete no-op — zero code paths touched
Validation runs BEFORE the loop, not inside it
Chain advancement uses LoopResult.branch_name (authoritative source from engine/DB)
performance: Validation reads each PRD file once (small JSON files, negligible)
style: Extract validation into a private function (validate_chain_branches)
Follow existing eprintln! pattern for diagnostics
Chain banner printed once at batch start, not per-PRD

userStories.title: Implement chain logic in batch.rs: validation, tracking, stop-on-failure

acceptanceCriteria: Integration test in worktree.rs tests: test_ensure_worktree_chain_preserves_commits
Test creates a temp git repo with initial commit on main
Test creates branch 'feat/phase-1' via ensure_worktree (start_point=None)
Test makes a unique commit in the phase-1 worktree (e.g., writes 'phase1-marker.txt')
Test creates branch 'feat/phase-2' via ensure_worktree (start_point=Some('feat/phase-1'))
Test verifies phase-2 worktree contains 'phase1-marker.txt' (file existence check)
Test verifies phase-2's git log contains phase-1's commit message
Known-bad: if start_point is silently ignored, phase-2 would NOT contain phase1-marker.txt — this test catches it
Negative: phase-2 must NOT be just a detached HEAD or checkout of phase-1 — it must be a NEW branch
Test verifies `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` in phase-2 worktree returns 'feat/phase-2'
userStories.description:
Write an integration test that verifies the core chaining mechanism at the git level: creating a worktree with start_point from another branch, and verifying the resulting branch contains the source branch's commits.

DO: Create temp git repo, make commits on branch-a, create branch-b from branch-a using ensure_worktree(start_point=Some('branch-a')), verify branch-b's git log contains branch-a's unique commit.
DO NOT: Call run_loop or run_batch — this tests the git mechanism, not the batch orchestration.

Edge cases:
- Branch-a has multiple commits: branch-b should contain all of them
- Branch-a diverges from main: branch-b should have branch-a's divergent commits but not need main

Known-bad to guard against:
- Testing only that the worktree was created (exists on disk) without verifying the commit history — would pass even if start_point was silently ignored
edgeCases: Phase-1 branch has commits that diverge from main
Phase-2 must have its own branch name, not be a checkout of phase-1

failureModes.expectedBehavior: phase1-marker.txt would be missing in phase-2 — test catches this

userStories.notes: Test helper: setup_git_repo_with_file() in test_utils.rs creates a git repo with initial commit. To make commits in a worktree: use std::process::Command for git add/commit in the worktree path. To check git log: use `git log --oneline` in the worktree and check for commit message substring. Pattern: follow test_ensure_worktree_creates_new_worktree (line 494) for setup structure.

correctness: Must verify COMMIT HISTORY, not just branch creation
Must verify the branch NAME is correct (not detached HEAD)
performance: Test should complete in <2s (git operations on temp dir are fast)
style: Use descriptive assert messages explaining what went wrong

userStories.title: Integration test: verify git-level chaining works end-to-end

acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication (DRY principle)
Functions under 30 lines (flag complex ones)
Clear separation of concerns
Code follows existing project patterns
validate_chain_branches is well-factored and testable independently
userStories.description:
Review all implementation for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and maintainability.

DO: Check for duplicated logic, functions >30 lines, tight coupling between modules.
DO NOT: Skip — this gate catches issues before the final review.
userStories.notes: Issues found: (1) 4x repeated BatchResult early-exit struct in run_batch — DRY violation; (2) 2x near-identical 'push remaining as skipped' loops — DRY violation; (3) run_batch is ~260 lines, cleanup_worktree_after_prd is ~64 lines with 8 params. Tasks REFACTOR-FIX-001 and REFACTOR-FIX-002 added to address these. validate_chain_branches (32 lines) is well-factored and testable — no change needed.
userStories.title: Review for refactoring opportunities

acceptanceCriteria: Extract `fn batch_fail_early() -> BatchResult` helper used in all 4 early-exit sites in run_batch
Extract a helper to push remaining pairs as skipped PrdRunResults (used in stop-signal and chain-stop-on-failure paths)
No behavioral changes — same error paths, same exit codes
All existing tests pass unchanged
cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test all clean
userStories.description:
Fix DRY violations in batch.rs:

1. The early-exit `BatchResult { succeeded: 0, failed: 1, skipped: 0, results: vec![] }` is repeated 4 times in run_batch (lines ~283, ~295, ~327, ~340). Extract `fn batch_fail_early() -> BatchResult`.

2. The 'push remaining as skipped' loop appears twice (stop-signal path ~368 and chain-stop-on-failure path ~452). Both iterate over remaining pairs and push PrdRunResult{skipped:true,...}. Extract a helper `fn push_remaining_skipped(results: &mut Vec<PrdRunResult>, pairs: &[(PathBuf, PathBuf)], from: usize, skipped: &mut usize)`.

DO: Pure refactor — no behavior changes, no new logic.
DO NOT: Change error messages or add new functionality.
userStories.title: Fix DRY violations in batch.rs: early-exit helper and skipped-PRD push

acceptanceCriteria: Extract `fn collect_prd_files(patterns: &[String]) -> TaskMgrResult<Vec<PathBuf>>` from run_batch (handles pattern expansion, dedup, sort — currently ~40 lines)
Extract `fn print_batch_summary(results: &[PrdRunResult], total: usize, succeeded: usize, failed: usize, skipped: usize, chain: bool)` from run_batch (~30 lines)
run_batch body is reduced to orchestration flow only
Consider replacing 8-arg cleanup_worktree_after_prd with a CleanupContext struct if it simplifies the call sites
All existing tests pass unchanged
cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test all clean
userStories.description:
Break down run_batch (~260 lines) and cleanup_worktree_after_prd (~64 lines, 8 params).

run_batch responsibilities to extract:
- Pattern expansion + dedup: `collect_prd_files(patterns)` → `TaskMgrResult<Vec<PathBuf>>`
- Summary printing: `print_batch_summary(results, total, succeeded, failed, skipped, chain)`

For cleanup_worktree_after_prd: the #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] suppression is a code smell. Evaluate whether a `WorktreeCleanupContext` struct holding (yes, keep_worktrees, cleanup_worktree, chain) reduces noise at call sites. Only introduce if it genuinely simplifies — avoid over-engineering.

DO: Pure refactor — no behavior changes.
DO NOT: Add new logic or change existing function signatures beyond the extractions.
userStories.title: Break run_batch into sub-functions; address cleanup_worktree_after_prd arg count

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: --chain flag flows CLI → main.rs → run_batch → LoopRunConfig.chain_base → ensure_worktree start_point
Positive: LoopResult.branch_name flows engine.rs → batch.rs chain_base tracking
Positive: All errors propagated with context (map_err, not unwrap)
Positive: cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check all clean
Security: -- separator present in git worktree add args before start_point
Negative: No unwrap() in production code paths
Negative: chain=false must not touch any validation or tracking code paths
Negative: No dead code warnings for new code
Task update: Remaining tasks reviewed and updated based on implementation learnings
If issues found: FIX-xxx tasks added to JSON with priority 50-97
userStories.description:
Review all implementation for quality, security, integration wiring, and documentation needs. Run full test suite.

DO: Check every new function for error handling, check callers still work, verify no dead code, verify the -- separator is present in git args, verify chain=false is truly a no-op.
DO NOT: Skip the integration wiring check — ensure --chain flag flows from CLI through main.rs through batch.rs through engine.rs to worktree.rs.
userStories.notes: Clean review. Full wiring path verified: CLI --chain → main.rs → run_batch(chain) → LoopRunConfig.chain_base → engine.rs ensure_worktree(chain_base.as_deref()) → worktree.rs git args with -- separator. Reverse flow: LoopResult.branch_name → batch.rs chain_base advancement. All 14 early-return LoopResult sites set branch_name: None. chain=false is a complete no-op. No unwrap() in production paths. No dead code. All 2496 tests pass.
userStories.title: Code review + final verification
